10.response: {"Q0":"No, not at all. I found the instructions incredibly useful and clear. I'm a visual learner so the way this study was set up was so perfect. I really appreciated the trial tasks as well. Those helped me immensely in understanding the instructions and game before partaking in the actual tasks."}

6.response: {"Q0":"No, because that gem was unable to be sold."}

5.response: {"Q0":"No. I didn't want to waste my resources when I couldn't sell that particular gem anymore."}

11.response: {"Q0":"I think this study might examine how quickly people learn to prioritize things to maximize their earning potentials. "}

13.response: {"Q0":"I really enjoyed this game/study! The study was highly engaging. It really forced me to focus and give each round my complete attention. I'm highly competitive and always want to outdo myself and this game definitely brought that competitive side out of me. I did mess up a few different times, but I think I did okay overall! I'm really glad I had the opportunity to take part in this study. Amazing!"}

0.response: {"Q0":"I don't believe I had any particular strategy. I'm just hypercompetitive. I took each round really seriously and kept reminding myself not to click on anything when I saw the yellow planet, and then the same thing with the blue planet near the end of the game. I did mess up a few times but my only real strategy was to remain as focused as I could be."}

7.response: {"Q0":"The green ice didn't bother me at all. I didn't prioritize them during the meteor shower but I still saw them as a good investment. Digging them up on planets added a new element of surprise to the game that I really enjoyed."}

9.response: {"Q0":"Yes, I was way more focused on the selecting of galaxies task. That's because of the shorter time limit which demanded more of my attention to try selecting the best option for the highest reward. The planet task was relatively simple once you learned the order of the dirt, wood, and boulder keys. "}

1.response: {"Q0":"I don't feel like I had any real strategy, nor do I feel like that changed over the course of the game."}

2.response: {"Q0":"I believe I found gems on these planets close to half of the time that I dug. So maybe like 55% of the time."}

8.response: {"Q0":"This was the toughest part of the game, in my opinion. I felt pressured to make a  choice because of the time limit. So my only guide here was to make a choice. It didn't even matter if the choice was bad. As long as I made a choice so that I wouldn't get timed out and have that result in a non-answer. In the choices that I did make, I usually tried to choose the highest amount with the highest probability. But, again, due to the time limit, that didn't always happen because I didn't always choose the highest amount."}

3.response: {"Q0":"The Blue astronaut left once for me. After they left, I made sure to notice every time the Blue planet came up and that made me refrain from digging on that planet. I did mess up, once or twice, with removing a layer or two from a Blue planet after that astronaut had left. But I never fully dug on a Blue planet once that astronaut left. "}

4.response: {"Q0":"Yes. It made that particular gem worthless to me. So there was no point in digging on that planet anymore."}
